title: Aluminium alloy blanks with local flash annealing
abstract: The invention concerns a method for improving aluminium alloy blank tensile yield stress and formability comprising the successive steps of : providing a 6xxx series aluminium alloy slab; optionally homogenizing said slab; hot rolling and optionally cold rolling the slab to obtain a sheet; solution heat treating and quenching said sheet; cold rolling said sheet with at least 20 % cold work reduction; cutting said sheet into blanks; flash annealing a portion of the flange of said blanks at a temperature between 360Â°C and 480Â°C for a time sufficient to obtain recrystallization of said portion of the flange and cool to a temperature of less than 100Â°C. The improved blanks and the stamped product and painted stamped products obtained by the method of the invention are particularly useful for automotive applications because of their high strength.
claims: Claims A method for improving aluminium alloy blank tensile yield stress and formability comprising the successive steps of: a) providing a 6xxx series aluminium alloy slab; b) optionally homogenizing said slab; c) hot rolling and optionally cold rolling the slab to obtain a sheet; d) solution heat treating and quenching said sheet; e) cold rolling said sheet with at least 20 % cold work reduction; f) cutting said sheet into blanks; g) flash annealing a portion of the flange of said blanks at a temperature between 360Â°C and 480Â°C for a time sufficient to obtain recrystallization of said portion of the flange and cool to a temperature of less than 100Â°C. 2. The method of claim 1 wherein after cold rolling step e) and prior to cutting step f) said sheet is annealed at a time and temperature sufficient to obtain an increase of elongation A% in the LT direction of at least 15% and a variation of tensile yield strength in the LT direction less than 15%. The method according to claim 1 or claim 2 wherein cold rolling step e) is at least 30 % cold work and preferably at least 50 % cold work. 4. The method according any of claims 1 to 3 wherein said sheet final thickness is 3 mm or less. 5. The method according to any of claims 1 to 4 wherein the cooling to a temperature of less than 100 Â°C in step g) is done at a cooling rate of at least 30Â°C/s. 6. The method according to any of claims 1 to 5 wherein flash annealing operation g) is repeated at least twice. 7. The method according to any of claims 1 to 6 wherein said aluminium series aluminium alloy is selected among AA6016, AA6111, AA6013 and AA6056. 8. The method according to any of claims 1 to 6 wherein said 6xxx series aluminium alloy comprise in wt Si : 0.7 - 1.0; Mg : 1.2 - 1.6; Cu : up to 0.8; Mn : up to 0.7; Zn up to 1; Fe up to 0.5 ; Ti : up to 0.15, rest aluminium and unavoidable impurities up to 0.05 and 0.15 total, and preferably Si : 0.7 - 0.9; Mg : 1.2 - 1.6; Cu :up to 0.3; Mn up to 0.3; Zn up to 0.05; Fe 0.1 - 0.4 ; Ti : Â°.01 - 0.05, rest aluminium and unavoidable impurities up to 0.05 and 0.15 total. 9. A locally recrystallized aluminium alloy blank obtainable according to the method of any of claims 1 to 8. 10. A stamped 6xxx series aluminium alloy product obtained by : placing the flange of a blank according to claim 9 within the blank holder of a press; stamping said blank to obtain a rough stamped product; removing the flange from said rough stamped product. 11. A stamped product according to claim 10 having a tensile yield strength in the LT direction of at least 250 MPa, preferably at least 290 MPa and more preferably at least 320 MPa. 12. A stamped product according to claim 10 or claim 11 characterized in that it is essentially non- recrystallized. 13. A stamped product according to any of claims 10 to 12 with a tensile yield strength in the LT direction at least 25% higher, preferably at least 50 % higher and more preferably at least 75 % higher than the tensile yield strength in the LT direction measured in T4-temper for a blank of the same alloy obtained by the same process steps a) to f) of the method of claim 1. 14. A stamped product according to any of claims 10 to 13 having after the painting line a tensile yield strength in the LT direction of at least 290 MPa, preferably at least 350 MPa, more preferably at least 400 MPa, and even more preferably at least 430 MPa. 15. The use of a stamped product according to any of claims 10 to 14 for automotive applications.
